en.summary: Imam al-Sadiq (peace be upon him) delivers a powerful exhortation on the imperative of continuous self-improvement. Addressed to the faithful gathered in the assembly of Sheikh al-Saduq in 368 AH, this teaching establishes a graduated scale: stagnation is a spiritual loss, regression is a curse, and the failure to pursue growth leads one closer to ruin. The hadith reflects a core Islamic ethical principle that a believer must constantly advance in knowledge, piety, and good deeds.

key_terms.مغبون: Magbun: deceived or at a loss, one who has been cheated in a transaction - here metaphorically indicating spiritual loss from wasting time
key_terms.ملعون: Mal'un: cursed or removed from God's mercy - a severe warning against regression
key_terms.النقصان: Nuqsan: decrease, decline, deterioration - the opposite of spiritual growth
key_terms.الزيادة: Ziyadah: increase, growth, improvement - referring to advancement in faith and virtue

en.seo_question: What did Imam al-Sadiq say about self-improvement and wasting time?

ur.summary: امام صادق علیہ السلام نے مسلسل خود اصلاحی کی اہمیت پر زور دیا۔ جمود خسارہ ہے، پسپائی لعنت ہے، اور ترقی کی کمی تنزلی کی طرف لے جاتی ہے۔

key_terms.مغبون: مغبون: دھوکہ دیا گیا، خسارے میں
key_terms.ملعون: ملعون: خدا کی رحمت سے دور

ur.seo_question: امام صادق علیہ السلام نے خود اصلاحی کے بارے میں کیا فرمایا؟

isnad_matn.isnad_ar: وَبِهَذَا الْإِسْنَادِ، قَالَ: قَالَ الصَّادِقُ (عَلَيْهِ السَّلَامُ):
isnad_matn.matn_ar: مَنِ اسْتَوَى يَوْمَاهُ فَهُوَ مَغْبُونٌ، وَمَنْ كَانَ آخِرُ يَوْمَيْهِ شَرَّهُمَا فَهُوَ مَلْعُونٌ، وَمَنْ لَمْ يَعْرِفِ الزِّيَادَةَ فِي نَفْسِهِ كَانَ إِلَى النُّقْصَانِ أَقْرَبَ، وَمَنْ كَانَ إِلَى النُّقْصَانِ أَقْرَبَ فَالْمَوْتُ خَيْرٌ لَهُ مِنَ الْحَيَاةِ

narrators.known_identity: Imam Ja'far al-Sadiq (AS), the sixth Imam (d. 148 AH)

key_phrases.phrase_en: death is better for him than life

similar_content_hints.description: A hadith attributed to the Prophet (SAWW) with similar wording: 'Whoever has two equal days is at a loss', widely reported in various collections.
Imam Ali (AS) in Nahj al-Balagha emphasizes self-reckoning (muhasaba) and daily self-examination.
Surah al-Asr (103:1-3) warns that mankind is in loss except those who believe and do good deeds - thematically parallel.
